title: Sorting
description: Sorting is fundamental to interview problem-solving. Master JavaScript's sort() with custom comparators, understand the numeric sort gotcha, and know how to sort complex data by specific keys.

sections.title: sort() with comparator functions
sections.explanation: JavaScript's sort() mutates the array in-place and takes an optional comparator. The comparator `(a, b) => a - b` sorts ascending, `(a, b) => b - a` sorts descending. Without a comparator, sort() converts elements to strings — a critical trap for numbers.

exercise.prompt: Sort `[30, 5, 20, 1, 10]` ascending with a comparator and print joined by commas. Then sort descending and print joined by commas.

validation.message: Use sort with an arrow function comparator

exercise.hint: a - b for ascending, b - a for descending.

sections.title: Sorting by a specific key
sections.explanation: For arrays of objects or tuples, pass a comparator that extracts the sort key. Sort by one property, or by multiple properties for tie-breaking. This pattern appears in problems like meeting intervals, task scheduling, and leaderboard ranking.

exercise.prompt: Sort intervals `[[3,6],[1,4],[2,5]]` by their start value (index 0). Print each interval as "start-end" on its own line.

validation.message: Use index [0] in the comparator to sort by start value

exercise.hint: Compare a[0] - b[0] to sort by the first element of each interval.

sections.title: Numeric sort gotcha
sections.explanation: The biggest JavaScript sort trap: without a comparator, sort() converts numbers to strings and sorts lexicographically. So [10, 9, 80] sorts to [10, 80, 9] because "10" < "80" < "9" as strings. Always provide a numeric comparator when sorting numbers.

exercise.prompt: Show the gotcha: sort `[10, 9, 80, 3]` without a comparator and print joined by commas. Then sort correctly with a numeric comparator and print joined by commas.

validation.message: String sort gives 10,3,80,9. Numeric sort gives 3,9,10,80
Use (a, b) => a - b for correct numeric sorting

exercise.hint: Default sort is lexicographic — gives wrong results for numbers. Use a - b.
